title: X (Twitter) API
summary: The full X API surface as MCP tools — reads, and OAuth1 user-context writes that act as your X account.

secrets.label: X app API key
secrets.hint: developer.x.com → your app → Keys and tokens → 'API Key and Secret'

secrets.label: X app API key secret
secrets.hint: same card as the API key

secrets.label: X access token
secrets.hint: 'Access Token and Secret' card — generate AFTER the app's user-authentication permission is set to read+write

secrets.label: X access token secret
secrets.hint: same card as the access token

notes: Writes act as YOUR X account — consider gating the write/delete group in the manage panel.
Read-only alternative: skip the four OAuth values and set X_BEARER_TOKEN (app-only bearer) instead.
Returns untrusted external content: the guardrail sidecar starts automatically with this tool.
